title: hieracium nobile subsp. bartlingii
intro: Hieracium nobile subsp. bartlingii, also known as Hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health issues such as wound healing, digestive problems, and respiratory infections. The health benefits of Hieracium nobile subsp. bartlingii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, as well as its use as a natural diuretic. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
